question: Find all airlines that have at least 10 flights.
gold_sql: SELECT T1.Airline FROM AIRLINES AS T1 JOIN FLIGHTS AS T2 ON T1.uid  =  T2.Airline GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING count(*)  >  10
gold_sql_original: SELECT T1.Airline FROM AIRLINES AS T1 JOIN FLIGHTS AS T2 ON T1.uid  =  T2.Airline GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING count(*)  >  10
gold_sql_canonical: SELECT T1.Airline FROM airlines AS T1 JOIN flights AS T2 ON T1.uid  =  T2.Airline GROUP BY T1.Airline HAVING count(*)  >  10
agent_sql: SELECT airlines.Airline AS airline_name, COUNT(*) AS flight_count FROM airlines JOIN flights ON airlines.uid = flights.Airline GROUP BY airlines.Airline LIMIT 100
safe_sql: SELECT airlines.Airline AS airline_name, COUNT(*) AS flight_count FROM airlines JOIN flights ON airlines.uid = flights.Airline GROUP BY airlines.Airline LIMIT 100
agent_answer: The query identified 12 airlines that each have at least 10 flights. All 12 airlines in the result have exactly 100 flights — including United Airlines, US Airways, Delta Airlines, Southwest Airlines, and American Airlines (shown in the sampled rows). The full list contains 12 distinct airlines, as confirmed by the executed query and result profile.
reason: Result mismatch: Column count mismatch: gold=1, agent=2
